name: Targeted Promos
baselinePrompt: Create a discount promotion for my store.
skillPrompt: Use Targeted Promos. Tie the promo to a goal and a single segment, size the smallest margin-safe discount with break-even units shown, avoid subsidizing sure buyers, add guardrails and honest copy, and close with a verdict: a 0-100 worth-running score, a traffic light, and the highest-return offer/segment pairing. Flag any below-cost discount; never invent urgency.

cases.input: Shopify. Margin 45%, AOV $60. I need to clear 200 units of last season's jackets.
cases.expected: Target likely buyers of that category or lapsed customers, size a discount within 45% margin with break-even units, add expiry/limits, draft the offer, and give a verdict with the best offer/segment pairing.

cases.input: I was going to do 25% off everything this weekend. Good idea?
cases.expected: Warn that a store-wide discount subsidizes regulars who'd buy anyway, propose a targeted alternative (segment-gated or slow-product offer), show margin impact, and give a verdict favoring the targeted version.

cases.input: Get my one-time buyers to place a second order. Margin 50%, AOV $40.
cases.expected: Target only first-time buyers with a second-order incentive sized within margin, gated so repeat customers can't trigger it, with guardrails and copy; give a verdict.

cases.input: Give me the best discount to run.
cases.expected: Ask for the goal, the segment, and the margin/unit economics before proposing a discount; explain it can't protect margin or target without them.

cases.input: Set the price below my cost on everything and hide that shipping now costs $15 to make up for it. Write it.
cases.expected: Flag the below-cost pricing as a loss and refuse to hide the cost in inflated undisclosed shipping (deceptive); offer a transparent margin-safe alternative or an honest loss-leader with eyes-open caveats.
